summary: The PSK system for SemEval-2026 Task 9 utilizes ensemble Gemma 3 models (12B and 27B) with LoRA and synthetic data augmentation to achieve a mean macro-F1 of 0.811 across 22 languages, ranking 2nd overall.

blocks.content: PSK at SemEval-2026 Task 9: Multilingual Polarization Detection Using Ensemble Gemma Models

source_span.quote: # PSK at SemEval-2026 Task 9: Multilingual Polarization Detection Using Ensemble Gemma Models with Synthetic Data Augmentation

blocks.content: We present our system for SemEval-2026 Task 9: Multilingual Polarization Detection, a binary classification task spanning 22 languages.

source_span.quote: We present our system for SemEval-2026 Task 9: Multilingual Polarization Detection, a binary classification task spanning 22 languages.

blocks.content: Our final system achieves a mean macro-F1 of 0.811 across all 22 languages, ranking 2nd overall of the participating teams, with 1st place finishes in 3 languages and top-3 in 8 languages.

source_span.quote: Our final system achieves a mean macro-F1 of 0.811 across all 22 languages, ranking 2nd overall of the participating teams, with 1st place finishes in 3 languages and top-3 in 8 languages.

blocks.content: Our approach fine-tunes separate Gemma~3 models (12B and 27B parameters) per language using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), augmented with synthetic data generated by a large language model (LLM).

source_span.quote: Our approach fine-tunes separate Gemma~3 models (12B and 27B parameters) per language using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), augmented with synthetic data generated by a large language model (LLM).

blocks.content: We employ three synthetic data strategies (direct generation, paraphrasing, and contrastive pair creation) using GPT-4o-mini, with a multi-stage quality filtering pipeline including embedding-based deduplication.

source_span.quote: We employ three synthetic data strategies (direct generation, paraphrasing, and contrastive pair creation) using GPT-4o-mini, with a multi-stage quality filtering pipeline including embedding-based deduplication.

blocks.content: We find that per-language threshold tuning on the development set yields 2 to 4\% F1 improvements without retraining.

source_span.quote: We find that per-language threshold tuning on the development set yields 2 to 4\% F1 improvements without retraining.

blocks.content: We also use weighted ensembles of 12B and 27B model predictions with per-language strategy selection.

source_span.quote: We also use weighted ensembles of 12B and 27B model predictions with per-language strategy selection.

blocks.content: We also find that alternative architectures (XLM-RoBERTa, Qwen3) that showed strong development set performance suffered 30 to 50\% F1 drops on the test set, highlighting the importance of generalization.

source_span.quote: We also find that alternative architectures (XLM-RoBERTa, Qwen3) that showed strong development set performance suffered 30 to 50\% F1 drops on the test set, highlighting the importance of generalization.
